source_file: The corporate social credit system in China and its transnational impact.txt
text:
social forces, but possessing global orientations and agendas.’55 The aims and
eﬀects of the CSCS go beyond ﬁnancial credit ratings or quality control. It
will be seen in the following that it might have a direct impact on corporate
behaviour, not only within the PRC but also externally within the sphere of
corporations and the contest over their regulation.
   The external impact of the CSCS builds on the characteristics of transna-
tional law as a political project. It does not necessarily create a legal order
above the national but it can have a direct impact on ‘individuals, groups,
local and national governments at multiple levels of activity’.56 Simul-
taneously, transnational law as a method oﬀers a valuable analytical frame-
work to describe the CSCS and makes the political project and its
‘mechanism’ visible. It captures subtle and evolving relationships between
national, international and transnational legality as well as the inﬂuences
of diverse actors.57 It ‘extends across, and thereby links as well as transcends,
diﬀerent (territorial) “levels”’.58
